reasoning:
To evaluate this question, we must estimate the probability that Wei Yi’s FIDE Elo rating, as listed on the Wikipedia 'FIDE rankings' page, will be at least 1% higher than his rating on November 9, 2025.
1. The Wikipedia page reflects the FIDE monthly ratings. On Nov 1, 2025, Wei Yi's rating was 2753. This value will also be the rating on Nov 9, 2025, as FIDE updates classical ratings monthly on the 1st.
2. An increase of 1% equates to gaining ~28 Elo points, requiring Wei Yi to reach 2781. 
3. For November 16, 2025: The Wikipedia page will predictably not have changed its underlying December data since the FIDE list only drops on the 1st of every month. The rating will be identical (2753), rendering the probability effectively 0 (capped at the 0.05 minimum).
4. For December 9, 2025: This falls under the December 1st FIDE list. To gain 28 points in one rating period, Wei Yi would have needed a historic performance at the FIDE World Cup 2025 (e.g., scoring +6 or +7 wins against Elite GMs without a loss in Classical format). Such a surge at the top level is extremely rare, especially since matches often go to rapid tiebreaks (which don't impact Classical Elo).
5. For February 7, 2026: Wei Yi has until the February FIDE list (allowing participation in other major winter events, like Tata Steel) to accumulate these 28 points. While possible over a broader horizon, achieving an Elo of 2781 (+28 points) represents passing his peak rating by a considerable margin. Our uncertainty marginally widens over this longer timeline.
